description: A graphic styled as a Chicago Tribune online news article header on a light gray background. At top is the Chicago Tribune masthead in its blue blackletter (Old English) font, with a thin rule beneath it and a breadcrumb 'NEWS > CRIME AND PUBLIC SAFETY' in gray sans-serif. Below, in the Tribune's black serif headline font, is the headline 'Chicago police arrest [Rogers Park man] in Loyola slaying'; the words 'Rogers Park man' are struck through with a hand-drawn red line, and red italic bold capitalized text 'TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN' is placed above that line. As shown it reads: 'Chicago police arrest TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN [Rogers Park man, struck through] in Loyola slaying.' The tweet text states the Tribune made a 'BLATANT OMISSION' regarding the murder of Sheridan Gorman.
notable_text: Chicago Tribune | NEWS > CRIME AND PUBLIC SAFETY | Chicago police arrest TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN [Rogers Park man, struck through] in Loyola slaying
